steps.title: Build line j from two points
steps.content: Line $k$ is $y=8x+7$, so its slope is $8$. Since line $j$ is parallel to $k$, line $j$ also has slope $8$. The point $(0,15)$ is on line $j$; moving $1$ unit right raises $y$ by $8$, so a second point is $(1,23)$.

steps.title: Fit line j in Desmos
steps.content: Enter $x_1=[0,1]$ and $y_1=[15,23]$, then run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=8$ and $b=15$, so the equation is $y=8x+15$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = 15x + 7$: Slope $= 15 eq 8$. Not parallel to $k$. ✗
  • Choice B — $y = -15x + 7$: Slope $= -15 eq 8$. Not parallel to $k$. ✗
  • Choice C — $y = 8x + 15$: Slope $= 8$ (parallel to $k$) and passes through $(0,15)$. ✓
  • Choice D — $y = -8x + 15$: Slope $= -8 eq 8$. Not parallel to $k$ (perpendicular slopes would be $- rac{1}{8}$, not $-8$). ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
